title: [Tauros] Battle Guide
summary: A [Normal]-type physical cleaner that turns [sheer-force] into huge functional damage on any secondary-effect move at 110 Speed.
strengths: [sheer-force] boosts every useful cove[rage] move by 1.3x
110 Speed outruns the vast majority of metagame threats
weaknesses: Thin 75/95/70 bulk folds to any priority or focused attack
[Normal] STAB is walled by ghost types entirely

recommendedItems.reason: Locks into the cleaner role with guaranteed speed tier
Raises [Normal] STAB damage with no drawback
Offsets thin bulk and secures one guaranteed attack

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Tauros] is a [Normal] physical attacker whose entire case in the Champions format hinges on [sheer-force]. 100 Attack and 110 Speed read as middling, but [sheer-force] boosts [rock-slide], [iron-head], [earthquake], and [body-slam] by a flat 30 percent, which turns [Tauros] into a slippery cleaner. The role is simple: come in late, punch out whatever is left, don't get walled.

## Strengths
The [sheer-force] boost applies to every attack you actually want to use, so [Tauros] produces damage comparable to much higher base Attack stats. 110 Speed is above the tier of most unboosted offensive Pokemon, and the cove[rage] pool is broad enough that [Tauros] flexes into whichever slot the team needs.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Tauros]'s bulk is thin — 75/95/70 is fragile on the special side, which means any special attacker is a legitimate [revenge] threat. The [Normal] STAB also hits ghost types for zero damage, so walls like gengar or golurk completely stop it unless you bring a cove[rage] move. [Rock] and [Steel] walls demand careful cove[rage] prediction. Any team running a priority user ([extreme-speed], [bullet-punch]) is also a real threat.

## Recommended Moveset
[rock-slide] is the locked slot for [Flying] and [Fire] cove[rage] with the [sheer-force] boost. [iron-head] punishes Fairies and [Rock] walls while still getting the ability boost. [earthquake] is mandatory cove[rage] for [Steel] and [Electric] pivots that try to [absorb] [rock-slide]. The last slot is [body-slam] as the [Normal] STAB — [sheer-force] removes the paralysis chance but keeps the 30 percent damage boost. [double-edge] is an alternative if you don't mind the re[coil] and want max raw damage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[choice-scarf] is the cleanest build — the locked move plus the speed tier makes [Tauros] a reliable [revenge] killer. [silk-scarf] raises [Normal] STAB damage with no drawback and preserves move flexibility, making it the best option for the four-move set. [focus-sash] is the panic option for a lead-like role. The ability must be [sheer-force] — [intimidate] and anger-point don't offer comparable damage output. Tera [Normal] maxes [body-slam], and Tera [Ground] flips [Electric] resistances while buffing [earthquake].

## Teammates & Synergy
[Tauros] absolutely requires a [Dark] partner to answer ghost walls that stop the [Normal] STAB. A dedicated breaker that removes [Rock] and [Steel] pivots — either a fighting attacker or a fast water — is also valuable. Pivoting support via [u-turn] or [volt-switch] creates the safe entry turns a cleaner needs, and speed control like [tailwind] lets [Tauros] work before a [choice-scarf] slot locks in. Intimidate or screens help keep the thin bulk alive on its entry turn.

## Play Patterns
Keep [Tauros] in the back as a fourth or fifth slot and bring it in once the oppo[sing] bench has lost its checks. The basic pattern is to lock into a [sing]le cove[rage] move that the current field can't [absorb] — [rock-slide] for [Flying], [iron-head] for [Fairy], [earthquake] for [Steel]. With [silk-scarf] keep your move pool flexible and pick the cove[rage] per entry. Save Tera for the moment a [Ghost] or priority move is clearly coming.
